texts.full_text: Jordanaires' Member Gordon Stoker Dies at 88 The member of Elvis Presley's backing vocal group passed away at the age of 88 because of long illness. Mar 28, 2013 AceShowbiz - Gordon Stoker, a member of vocal group The Jordanaires, passed away at the age of 88 on Wednesday, March 27, in his home in Brentwood. His son Alan said that Gordon passed away after a long illness. Gordon was in The Jordanaires alongside Neal Matthews Jr., Hoyt Hawkins and Culley Holt. Their vocals are featured in songs that belong to 1950s country music stars such as Patsy Cline's "Crazy" or Jim Reeves' "Four Walls". They also worked with Elvis Presley for more than a decade. The group was originally formed by Matthews, Stoker, Hoyt Hawkins and Culley Holt. After Holt left the band in 1954 to become a minister, he was replaced by Hugh Jarrett who later quit because of his drinking problem. The remaining members then recruited Ray Walker as their bass singer after they heard him singing with another group. Stoker's son said that even though Gordon had been retired from performing for some time now due to health issues, "he still loved getting out and seeing the fans." He added that Stoker was planning on going back into studio soon. Alan also revealed his father's last performance which took place in 2012 at a private event. In 2001, The Jordanaires were given a Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award by National Academy of Recording Arts & Sciences for their contribution to rock and roll music. They are the only vocal group that was awarded with such honor

texts.full_text: , including 4 weeks of nutrition education, combined with an increase in the content and bioavailability of dietary iron for 22 weeks was carried out in 34 intervention and 34 control adolescent girls suffering from mild iron deficiency anemia in Benin, West Africa. The intervention was designed to increase dietary iron intake and bioavailability, and to educate the girls and their families about the importance and sources of iron in their diets. The control group received standard of care, which was an iron and folic acid supplement, and standard dietary advice. The primary outcomes of the study were to assess the iron status and the dietary intake of iron in the girls. The study was a randomized, controlled, and blinded trial, and the outcomes were assessed at the baseline, at the end of the education period, and at the end of the dietary intervention period. The district court, after a jury trial, found the appellant guilty of the offenses charged. On appeal, the appellant raises several arguments, primarily focusing on the sufficiency of the evidence and the alleged errors during the trial. The appellee, the United States of America, argues that the evidence was sufficient to support the jury's verdict and that the appellant's arguments regarding trial errors are without merit. The record and the briefs of the parties having been examined, and the arguments having been heard, and the court having arrived at a decision, it is hereby ORDERED, ADJUDGED, AND DECREED that the judgment of the district court be and hereby is AFFIRMED. texts.full_text: and methods for implementing a communications protocol for a storage media interface. Related Disclosure A communications protocol for a storage media interface specifies how a controller on a storage medium receives commands for processing from a host over an interface. To enable faster adoption and interoperability, the communication protocols are often standardized or follow widely accepted conventions to ensure that different devices can communicate effectively without proprietary barriers. In existing systems, various challenges arise when implementing these communications protocols due to differences in hardware capabilities across storage media types (e.g., SSDs vs. HDDs), varying performance requirements for read/write operations, and the need for efficient error handling mechanisms during data transfer processes. Additionally, as technology advances, there is a growing demand for more sophisticated features such as enhanced security measures, support for new command sets that can leverage advanced storage capabilities like NVMe (Non-Volatile Memory Express) over fabrics in networked environments. To address these challenges and to meet the evolving needs of modern data centers and consumer devices alike, this disclosure introduces an improved communications protocol designed specifically with flexibility, scalability, performance optimization, and security enhancements at its core. The new approach aims not only to streamline command processing but also to provide a robust framework for future innovations in storage media interfaces. The disclosed system includes mechanisms that: - Optimize Command Queuing: Implementing advanced queuing techniques tailored to the specific characteristics of different types of non-volatile and volatile memory, ensuring efficient use of bandwidth while minimizing latency. - Enhance Error Recovery Processes: Introducing more sophisticated error detection algorithms alongside proactive recovery strategies designed to minimize data loss or corruption during transmission. - Support for Advanced Security Features: Incorporating encryption protocols at the protocol level that can be seamlessly integrated with existing security frameworks, ensuring secure communication between host and storage devices without compromising performance.

texts.full_text: caught in the crossfire. His images reveal the stark reality of life during wartime—the fear, the exhaustion, and the moments of fleeting normalcy amidst chaos. In this particular photo series, Smith documented a group of U.S. Marines destroying a Japanese blockhouse on Iwo Jima. The scene captured in his lens shows the intense confrontation between the two opposing forces, with the explosive force of war vividly displayed against the backdrop of the Pacific island's rugged terrain. Smith's photos not only provided a visual record of this pivotal moment in World War II but also served as a powerful commentary on the brutality and futility of warfare. They challenge viewers to reflect on the consequences of conflict and to consider the often hidden costs of war, beyond the statistics and headlines. LIFE.com's presentation of these previously unpublished photos offers a fresh perspective on Smith's work and the historical events they depict. By bringing attention to images that were once overlooked or forgotten, it encourages a deeper engagement with history and prompts viewers to question their assumptions about what war truly entails. These photographs serve as a poignant reminder of the sacrifices made by those who fought in World War II, as well as a call for empathy and understanding towards all individuals affected by conflict. In this paper, we conduct a “thought experiment” to redesign the mobile communications system to provide a high level of security and privacy for the users of the system. We discuss the important requirements and how a different architecture might successfully satisfy them. In doing so, we hope to illuminate the possibilities for secure and private systems, as well as explore their real limits.' author: - | G. Winfield Treese\ Serissa Research, Inc.\ and\ Edward R. Introduction The rapid proliferation of mobile communication technologies has transformed global connectivity, enabling real-time voice, data, and multimedia exchange across vast distances. However, this ubiquity has also exposed critical vulnerabilities in the security and privacy of these systems. Modern mobile networks, while robust in many respects, were not originally designed with end-to-end security as a primary concern. Instead, they evolved incrementally, layering new capabilities onto legacy infrastructures that prioritized scalability, cost-efficiency, and interoperability over user privacy. As a result, today’s systems remain susceptible to interception, surveillance, and exploitation by both state and non-state actors. The challenges to securing mobile communications are multifaceted. Let's delve into examples of subharmonic, not plurisubharmonic functions, as well as a function that satisfies both properties. ### Examples 1. Subharmonic but Not Plurisubharmonic: Consider \( u(z_1) = |z_1|^2 \). This is defined on the complex plane and can be extended to higher dimensions by setting it independent of other variables, i.e., \( u(z_1, z_2, ..., z_n) = |z_1|^2 \). - Subharmonic: The function \( u(z_1) = |z_1|^2 \) is subharmonic because its Laplacian (trace of the complex Hessian matrix in one dimension): $$ \Delta_{\mathbb{C}} u = 4 \frac{\partial^2}{\partial z_1 \partial \bar{z}_1} |z_1|^2 = 4 $$ is positive. - Not Plurisubharmonic: To check if \( u(z_1, ..., z_n) = |z_1|^2 \) is plurisubharmonic in higher dimensions, we need to compute the complex Hessian matrix: $$ \left( \frac{\partial^2 u}{\partial z_j \partial \bar{z}_k} \right)_{j,k=1,\ldots,n}. $$ For \( j = k = 1 \): $$ \frac{\partial^2 |z_1|^2}{\partial z_1 \partial \bar{z}_1} = 1. $$ All other entries are zero: $$ \left( \begin{array}{cccc} 1 & 0 & \cdots & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & \cdots & 0 \\ \vdots & \vdots & \ddots & \vdots \\ 0 & 0 & \cdots & 0 \end{array} \right). $$ This matrix is not positive semi-definite because it has a zero eigenvalue, hence \( u(z_1) = |z_1|^2 \) (extended to higher dimensions as described) is subharmonic but not plurisubharmonic. 2. According to the press release, Ms. Braden-Harder was recognized for leading her company through an IPO on the Australian stock exchange earlier this year. She started the Butler Hill Group in 1993 before merging it with Australian-based Appen in 2010. The company now reaches more than 50 countries, with offices in San Francisco and Seattle as well as Europe and Asia, and employs 150 people. In addition to earning an MS in computer science from Courant in 1991, she received a BS in computer science from Purdue University in 1982. Man of the Hour - Professor emeritus Louis Nirenberg April 14, 2015 It has been an eventful year for Professor Nirenberg, a Courant alumnus (MS, PhD '47) as well as a distinguished member of the faculty. The Institute celebrated his 90th birthday (a few months early) at a reception on October 17, 2014. We were pleased to be joined for the occasion by many of Louis' mathematical friends. We were then thrilled to learn on March 25 that Professor Nirenberg was named a 2015 Abel Prize winner with John F. Nash, Jr. of Princeton. The Norwegian Academy of Sciences & Letters, which confers the prize, cited Professors Nirenberg and Nash “for striking and seminal contributions to the theory of nonlinear partial differential equations and its applications to geometric analysis.” The Institute celebrated with a reception

texts.full_text: D) and CAPD and Kidney Transplantation (KT) Effects on AGE in the skin and peritoneum: CAPD: CAPD, the continuous dialysis of the peritoneal membrane, has the potential to reduce AGE levels in the skin and peritoneum of patients with CRF in the following ways: 1. Dialysis of AGE and AGE precursors (e.g., glucose and glucose metabolites): CAPD helps to reduce the levels of AGE precursors in the peritoneal cavity and, to some extent, in the systemic circulation. This can potentially reduce the formation of AGE in the peritoneal and systemic circulation and in the skin, the largest organ of the body, which is in constant contact with the systemic circulation and peritoneal cavity. 2. Improved peritoneal membrane function and AGE clearance: CAPD can improve peritoneal membrane function, potentially enhancing the clearance of AGE and AGE precursors, which can reduce the AGE burden in the peritoneal cavity and systemic circulation. Kidney Transplantation (KT): KT can potentially reduce AGE burden in the skin and peritoneal cavity, and improve AGE-related complications in the following ways: 1. Normalization of renal function and AGE precursors: A successful renal transplant can restore renal function and reduce the systemic and peritoneal AGE precursors, potentially reducing the formation of AGE in the skin and peritoneal cavity. 2. Improved systemic and peritoneal AGE clearance: A successful renal transplant can restore the clearance of AGE and AGE precursors, reducing the AGE burden in the systemic and peritoneal cavity. However, the effects of CAPD and KT on AGE in the skin and peritoneal cavity

texts.full_text: ou’ll see that he supports funding for Iron Dome. Maybe that’s because purely defensive weapons are more copacetic with his view of foreign entanglements or maybe it’s because, having tried and failed to convince conservatives that cutting aid could be good for America and for Israel, he’s decided to give in before 2016 rivals like Ted Cruz start paying attention to this issue. I tend to think he realized at some point that his larger project of cutting all foreign aid would face much greater resistance within the GOP if it included Israel, so by bowing to them on that point and building up some credibility with hawks now, they might be willing to support cutting aid for everyone but Israel later. Whatever the answer, though, if you’re skittish about Paul’s foreign policy you can take some comfort in the fact that, unlike his old man, he apparently can and will bend towards majority Republican opinion if crossing it imperils his career. In fact, read Leon Wolf’s post at Red State noting that this isn’t the first time Paul has flip-flopped on an issue for apparently political reasons. For most politicians, that would be a black mark. For Paul, who’s busy flipping towards mainstream conservatives, maybe it’s reassuring to the voters he needs. Update: This is, I think, the first time we’ve received a statement from Paul’s office

texts.full_text: The endothelin axis has been implicated in cancer growth, angiogenesis, and metastasis, but to the authors' knowledge the expression of endothelin genes has not been defined in renal cell carcinoma (RCC). Tissue specimens were harvested from both normal and tumor-affected regions at the time of radical nephrectomy from 35 patients with RCC (22 with clear cEll RCC [ccRCC] and 13 with papillary RCC [PRCC]). Real-time reverse transcriptase-polymerase chain reaction analysis determined the expression profile of the preproendothelins (PPET-1, PPET-2, and PPET-3), the endothelin receptors (ET(A) and ET(B)), and the endothelin-converting enzymes (ECE-1 and ECE-2). PPET-1 was found to be up-regulated in ccRCC when compared with PRCC. In contrast, both RCC subtypes demonstrated a significant decrease in expression of all other genes examined on this axis: ET(A), ETA/B ratio; ppets -3,-4 and ECEs

texts.full_text: e side. Another option is to go through a brokerage and buy wine futures, much like you would for soy beans or crude oil. However, these might turn out to be worthless if the wine doesn't appreciate fast enough. If you're determined to invest in wine, though, probably the most satisfying route is to build a cellar, get professional advice about filling it, and then hope for the best. If worse comes to worst, you can still drink your losses. SCOTT BERNARD NELSON is deputy business editor at The Oregonian and a freelance writer in Portland, Oregon. In an unprecedented breakthrough, the U.S. government's National Heart, Lung and Blood Institute (NHLBI) provided the first major multidisciplinary programmatic grant to study the effects of moderate wine consumption on cardiovascular health to the University of Alabama at Birmingham (UAB). A recent article in the Journal of Thrombosis and Haemostasis determined that drinkers of wine benefit from its cardioprotective effects, more so than those who drink beer or other spirits, and wine drinkers may also live longer. Funeral service was held October 3, 2007, in the Johnson- Smith Funeral Home Chapel, 207 S. Main St., Baltimore with Pastor Michael Herbert of Walnut Creek Baptist Church officiating. Burial followed in Betzer Cemetery. Memorial gifts may be given to Walnut Creek Baptist Church, PO Box 216, Baltimore, OH 43105. John Robert "Bob" Pickens John Robert "Bob" Pickens Sr., 68, of Thurston, died Sunday, Sept. 30, 2007. He was born Oct. 1, 1938, to the late John F. and Marie Nott Pickens. Bob was a simple man who loved music and bowling with his buddies. He was a United States Navy veteran on the USS Coral Sea and served in the Panama Canal Conflict.He was a member of the American Legion Post 11, Buckeye Lake Eagles 2801, Teamsters Local 413 and was a retired truck driver for the Kroger Co. with 40 years of service. He is survived by his sons, John (Tammy) Pickens Jr. of Groveport and Keith (fianceé Melissa) Pickens of Baltimore; daughter, Chanin (Scott) Tate of Lowgap, N.C.; grandchildren, Matthew (Julie), Jessica, Ryan, Brandon, Haley, Briana, Bobby, and Olivia; great-grandson, Ridge; former wife, Evalena and her children and grandchildren; sisters, Barbara Sherbourne, Carol Black, Judy (Tom) Buscemi and Katy Kurash; brother-in-law, Don Dreher; special friend, Woody Phillips; several nieces and nephews; and his dog, Buddy. He was preceded in death by his parents; grandson, Jeremy John Pickens; sister, Mary Dreher; and nephew, Joshua Pearson. Services will be at 7 p.m. Thursday at Frank e. Smith Funeral

texts.full_text: R) probe-sets. Probe-sets with M-values (log ratios) located beyond the upper or lower fence of the boxplot were considered as HR gene probe-sets. ### Estimation of the global median absolute value of Pearson Correlation Coefficient (PCC) {#Sec28} The bootstrap method \[[@CR34]\] was used to evaluate the median absolute value of PCCs between the expression levels of any two probe-sets among GeneChips. First, 10,000 non-redundant probe pairs were randomly selected, and the absolute PCC between each pair was computed. Based on these 10,000 PCC values, 100,000 bootstrap samples were built by sampling with replacement to measure the 95% confidence interval of the global median absolute value of PCC. Identification of rice Hsps {#Sec29} --------------------------- Rice candidate Hsps were selected from the Uniprot database. These sequences satisfied the following criteria: (1) they possessed the conserved domains of Hsps (Additional file [1](#MOESM1){ref-type="media"}: Table S1); (2) they were functionally annotated as Hsps or involved in similar biological processes; (3) the sequence length was in agreement with the molecular mass of different Hsp family members; (4) Evidence at RNA or protein expression level; and (5) they were identified as Hsps in the MSU Rice Genome Annotation Project. texts.full_text: The results showed that 82% of the transthoracic current was shunted by the thoracic cage, while 14% was shunted by the lungs. The remaining 4% (FC) is the portion that passed through the heart. There was good agreement between the two independent methods used to calculate FC. Analysis based on the model indicated that FC was 3.7%, whereas FC determined by direct measurement with calibrated electrodes was 4.2%. Therefore, the results of this study, in contrast to earlier estimates of FC, show that defibrillation in dogs is achieved by only 4%

texts.full_text: k is, "What would someone do with this???" Yesterday I read a blog written by someone that reminded me of myself. She loves being artsy and craftsy and is drawn to publications about creativity, in awe of the super talents and prolific work of so many others. Yet, like me, she feared looking too closely or spending too much time exploring others' work for fear that her own ideas and art would be influenced by the work of another artist. Then there's the part of me that is like a deer in headlights. I'm surrounded by artistic materials and, more often than not, have the time to be creative but do nothing. It's depressing. Today I will at least explore some resources for artistic prompts. I'm wasting time, wasting opportunity and need a nudge. OK, I need a really big kick and a shove. About Me I would rather be funny than thin (I am), and I would rather be charitable of heart than beautiful (I am), but wouldn't complain if I were offered the thin and beautiful too! creativity is something with which I struggle, either because my mind goes a mile and minute and I have a hard time focusing, or because I'm being pulled too many directions and distracted with real life. I blog to help me

Similarly, D-NAP also demonstrated competitive inhibition at higher doses but to a lesser extent compared to its L-enantiomer counterpart and wild-type peptide. The results indicate that while all three synthetic analogs (ADFN-9, D-SAL) share structural similarities suggesting potential for binding interactions similar or analogous to NAP's mechanism of action on tubulin polymerization dynamics; only ADNF-9 shows significant competition with native brain-derived peptides at concentrations above 10 μM. Though this may seem overwhelming, the game allows you to hire up to two assistants from the want-ads. Each aide specializes in different abilities such as perfect cutting, grilling, or influencing customers to order the most expensive dishes on the menu. Bear in mind, these assistants are not meant to be your slaves. Getting a handle on equal duty distribution will ensure a productive relationship. Surprisingly, some small recent studies reported better cognitive performance in SCH patients with comorbid cannabis use disorders (SCH + CUD) compared to other SCH patients. The aim of the present study was to investigate the residual impact of CUD and specific patterns of consumption on cognition in a larger sample of SCH + CUD patients. We administered a cognitive test battery to 34 SCH and 35 currently abstinent SCH + CUD patients. We explored the association between patterns of cannabis consumption and cognitive performance.
